codeReview.summary: The feature added a first-class `seeded_bootstrap` runtime mode and aligned diagnostics plus targeted inject tests around it, but the current repo state still does not close the intended runtime-bootstrap gap on the real default/local server path. The implementation remains tied to host/port heuristics in `src/app.ts`, and the live `src/server.ts` entrypoint still fails to expose seeded snapshots for the default/local bootstrap-only path described by the feature.

issues.description: `resolvePersistentSnapshotDatabaseUrl` gates persisted snapshot import on a hard-coded host/port allowlist (3102, 3000, 3100, plus a few loopback variants) and explicitly returns `null` for `port === 0`. The feature description calls for seeded-bootstrap semantics for bootstrap-only `buildApp({ startBackgroundJobs: false })` when persisted rows are the intended source, not just these specific listener profiles. Bootstrap-only app instances on other ports or ephemeral ports still skip persisted snapshot import entirely, so the runtime-mode contract is not actually first-class.

The server entrypoint only switches to bootstrap-only mode for the validation profile on `127.0.0.1:3102`. The worker handoff for this feature confirms that a live default/local server started with `PORT=3000 HOST=0.0.0.0 DATABASE_URL=:memory:` still reported `validation_override.mode: "off"`, `shared_market_snapshot.available: false`, and failed its first hot-endpoint probe, even though inject-based tests passed. Because the feature explicitly required broad default/local runtime behavior and the same seeded-bootstrap semantics on the manifest-managed validation API, this unresolved live-server drift means the runtime bootstrap gap is not fully closed in current repo state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission runtime notes mention earlier live-vs-inject validation drift, but they still do not capture the default/local bootstrap-specific drift discovered here: a live `src/server.ts` bootstrap-only server on port 3000 can diverge from `buildApp(...).inject()` for seeded snapshot exposure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature handoff interactive check for `coins-runtime-snapshot-exposure-prep` reports port 3000 live server diagnostics showing `validation_override.mode: "off"` and no shared snapshot while inject-based tests passed; `.factory/library/runtime.md` documents validation-surface drift generally but not this default/local bootstrap case.

summary: Fail: current repo state still does not deliver the promised broad bootstrap seeded-snapshot behavior on the real default/local server path, and the runtime-mode logic is still host/port-special-cased rather than first-class.
